objects.name: usp_AWLT_OneCSV_ConverterTest_1k
known_limitations: Cursor-based row-by-row processing at lines 918-943 -- rewrite as vectorized operation
Dynamic SQL (sp_executesql) at lines 931-933 with runtime predicates -- cannot auto-convert; use predicate-map pattern if predicates are finite
FOR JSON PATH at lines 865-874 -- convert to F.to_json(F.struct(...))
CROSS APPLY OPENJSON at lines 891-897 -- convert to F.from_json() with StructType schema
OBJECT_ID checks at lines 652,663,689,720,743,778,819,834,851,881,905 -- skip; DataFrame lifecycle handles cleanup
Table variable @tmp at line 932 -- convert to Python list or small DataFrame
Temp table indexes with INCLUDE columns at lines 684,715,814 -- use .repartition().cache() as proxy

conversion_levels.description: Depends on Level 0 objects (logging proc + CSV splitter)
conversion_levels.strategy: Sequential multi-step conversion, careful review of gotchas, flag cursor/dynamic SQL for manual review

conversion_instructions: Convert to a RunLogger class with a log_step() method, matching the PySpark style guide pattern
Line 29: @RunId uniqueidentifier -> Python uuid.uuid4() stored as str; generate in caller
Line 30: @ProcName sysname -> str parameter
Line 31-34: @StepName, @StepStatus, @Message, @Complete -> method params: step_name: str, step_status: str, message: str | None = None, complete: bool = False
Line 37: SET NOCOUNT ON -> skip entirely
Line 39-40: INSERT dbo.AWLT_Converter_RunLog -> append dict to an internal Python list (self._log: list[dict]); include RunId, ProcName, StepName, StepStatus, Message fields
Line 40: CASE WHEN @Complete=1 THEN SYSUTCDATETIME() ELSE NULL END -> use datetime.now(timezone.utc) if complete else None for CompletedUtc field
Line 40: SYSUTCDATETIME() -> datetime.now(timezone.utc)
Expose a get_log_df(spark) -> DataFrame method to materialize log as DataFrame if needed for output
usp_AWLT_Converter_Log.strategy: Direct conversion, single pass. Convert to RunLogger utility class.

conversion_instructions: Convert to a pure Python function: def split_csv_int(csv_str: str | None) -> list[int]
Line 47-48: RETURNS @t TABLE (ItemInt int NOT NULL) -> return list[int]
Line 51: COALESCE(@csv, N'') -> csv_str = csv_str or ''
Line 54: REPLACE(REPLACE(REPLACE(@x, CHAR(10), ','), CHAR(13), ','), ';', ',') -> use str.replace() chained: x = x.replace('\n', ',').replace('\r', ',').replace(';', ',')
Line 55: Strip trailing commas -> x = x.rstrip(',')
Lines 57-67: WHILE loop with CHARINDEX/SUBSTRING parsing -> replace entire loop with Pythonic: [int(t.strip()) for t in x.split(',') if t.strip() and t.strip().lstrip('-').isdigit()]
Line 63: TRY_CONVERT(int, @token) IS NOT NULL -> use try/except int() or isdigit() check

Also provide a DataFrame-returning variant: def split_csv_int_df(spark: SparkSession, csv_str: str | None) -> DataFrame that returns a single-column DataFrame with schema StructType([StructField('CustomerID', IntegerType(), False)]) for use in joins
ufn_SplitCsvInt.strategy: Direct conversion, single pass. Pure Python string splitting replaces entire WHILE loop.

usp_AWLT_OneCSV_ConverterTest_1k.dependency_signatures: class RunLogger: def __init__(self, run_id: str, proc_name: str); def log_step(self, step_name: str, step_status: str, message: str | None = None, complete: bool = False) -> None; def split_csv_int(csv_str: str | None) -> list[int]; def split_csv_int_df(spark: SparkSession, csv_str: str | None) -> DataFrame
conversion_instructions: STRUCTURE: Create @dataclass PipelineParams with fields: start_date: date | None = None, end_date: date | None = None, as_of_date: date | None = None, customer_ids_csv: str | None = None, region_filter: str | None = None, recent_months_window: int = 6, revenue_tolerance_pct: Decimal = Decimal('0.0100'), emit_debug: bool = False
STRUCTURE: Main function def run_pipeline(spark: SparkSession, params: PipelineParams) -> DataFrame with 11 step functions
Line 627: SET NOCOUNT ON -> skip
Line 629: DECLARE @RunId uniqueidentifier = NEWID() -> run_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
Line 630: @ProcName -> proc_name = 'usp_AWLT_OneCSV_ConverterTest_1k'
Line 633: String concat with COALESCE -> f-string: f'Params: Region={params.region_filter or "(all)"}'
Line 635: EXEC dbo.usp_AWLT_Converter_Log -> logger.log_step('START', 'START', start_msg)

Lines 638-644: Date normalization from SalesOrderHeader -> if params.start_date is None or params.end_date is None: date_range = spark.table('sales_order_header').select(F.min(F.col('OrderDate').cast('date')), F.max(F.col('OrderDate').cast('date'))).first(); then fill None values from row
Line 646: IF @AsOfDate IS NULL SET @AsOfDate = @EndDate -> as_of_date = params.as_of_date or end_date
Line 648: BEGIN TRY -> try:
STEP 1 - Customer Filter (lines 652-658):
Line 652: OBJECT_ID check -> skip entirely
Lines 654-656: SELECT DISTINCT ItemInt FROM ufn_SplitCsvInt -> customer_filter_df = split_csv_int_df(spark, params.customer_ids_csv)
Line 658: @HasCustomerFilter -> has_customer_filter = customer_filter_df.count() > 0
STEP 2 - Base Orders (lines 663-684):
Line 663: OBJECT_ID check -> skip
Line 668: CAST(h.OrderDate AS date) -> F.col('OrderDate').cast('date').alias('OrderDate')
Line 674: COALESCE(a.StateProvince, a.CountryRegion, 'Unknown Region') -> F.coalesce(F.col('StateProvince'), F.col('CountryRegion'), F.lit('Unknown Region')).alias('RegionName')
Line 676-678: LEFT JOIN Address -> .join(address_df, F.col('ShipToAddressID') == F.col('AddressID'), 'left')
Line 679: BETWEEN @StartDate AND @EndDate -> .where(F.col('OrderDate').cast('date').between(start_date, end_date))
Line 680: EXISTS subquery for customer filter -> if has_customer_filter: .join(customer_filter_df, on='CustomerID', how='left_semi') else no-op. Apply BEFORE the main join for predicate pushdown
Line 681: @RegionFilter IS NULL OR ... = @RegionFilter -> if params.region_filter: .where(F.col('RegionName') == params.region_filter)
GOTCHA #2: Line 681 string comparison with RegionFilter -- SQL Server is case-insensitive. Use F.lower() on both sides: F.lower(F.col('RegionName')) == params.region_filter.lower()
Line 683: CREATE CLUSTERED INDEX CX_Orders ON #Orders(CustomerID, OrderDate, SalesOrderID) -> .repartition('CustomerID').cache(); then .count()
Line 684: CREATE INDEX with INCLUDE -> skip (covered by repartition+cache)
STEP 3 - Line Details (lines 689-715):
Line 689: OBJECT_ID check -> skip
Lines 691-713: Multi-table JOIN -> chain: detail.join(orders_df, 'SalesOrderID').join(product, 'ProductID').join(category, on=..., how='left').join(model, on=..., how='left')
Line 700-702: Alias columns -> .alias('ProductName'), .alias('CategoryName'), .alias('ModelName')
Line 715: Index -> .repartition('SalesOrderID').cache()
STEP 4 - Path A Aggregates (lines 720-738):
Line 720: OBJECT_ID check -> skip
Line 724: COUNT_BIG(*) -> F.count('*').alias('OrderCount')
Lines 729-730: CASE WHEN OnlineOrderFlag=1 -> F.sum(F.when(F.col('OnlineOrderFlag') == 1, 1).otherwise(0)).alias('OnlineOrderCount')
Line 738: Index -> .repartition('CustomerID').cache()
STEP 5 - Path B Aggregates (lines 743-773):
Line 743: OBJECT_ID check -> skip
Lines 745-760: CTE 'L' -> assign to intermediate variable: line_agg = orders_df.join(lines_df, 'SalesOrderID').groupBy('CustomerID', 'SalesOrderID').agg(...)
Line 754: COUNT(DISTINCT l.ProductID) -> F.countDistinct('ProductID').alias('DistinctProducts')
Lines 761-771: Outer SELECT grouping CTE -> agg_b = line_agg.groupBy('CustomerID').agg(F.sum('LineTotalSum').alias('TotalRevenue_PathB'), ...)
Line 773: Index -> .repartition('CustomerID').cache()
STEP 6 - Customer Facts (lines 778-814):
Line 778: OBJECT_ID check -> skip
Line 782: c.FirstName + ' ' + c.LastName -> F.concat_ws(' ', F.col('FirstName'), F.col('LastName')).alias('CustomerName')

Line 793: CASE WHEN a.OrderCount > 0 THEN (a.TotalRevenue_PathA / CONVERT(decimal(19,4), a.OrderCount)) ELSE 0 END -> F.when(F.col('OrderCount') > 0, F.col('TotalRevenue_PathA') / F.col('OrderCount').cast(DecimalType(19, 4))).otherwise(F.lit(0)).alias('AvgOrderValue')
GOTCHA #1: Line 799: DATEDIFF(day, a.LastOrderDate, @AsOfDate) -> F.datediff(F.lit(as_of_date), F.col('LastOrderDate')) -- REVERSED argument order! SQL is (unit, start, end), PySpark is (end, start)
GOTCHA #1: Line 800: DATEDIFF(day, a.FirstOrderDate, a.LastOrderDate) -> F.datediff(F.col('LastOrderDate'), F.col('FirstOrderDate')) -- same reversal
Line 801: ABS(ISNULL(a,0) - ISNULL(b,0)) -> F.abs(F.coalesce(F.col('TotalRevenue_PathA'), F.lit(0)) - F.coalesce(F.col('TotalRevenue_PathB'), F.lit(0)))

Line 804: NULLIF(CONVERT(decimal(19,6), a.TotalRevenue_PathA), 0) -> use _nullif_zero helper: F.when(F.col('TotalRevenue_PathA').cast(DecimalType(19,6)) == 0, F.lit(None)).otherwise(F.col('TotalRevenue_PathA').cast(DecimalType(19,6)))
GOTCHA #5: Lines 793, 804: Decimal arithmetic with CONVERT(decimal(19,4/6)) -- ensure spark.sql.decimalOperations.allowPrecisionLoss=false is set. Use explicit .cast(DecimalType(19,4)) on operands
Line 813: Index -> .repartition('CustomerID').cache()
STEP 7 - RFM Scoring (lines 819-829):
Line 819: OBJECT_ID check -> skip
Line 823: NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY cf.RecencyDays ASC) -> F.ntile(5).over(Window.orderBy(F.col('RecencyDays').asc_nulls_first())).alias('R_Score')
GOTCHA #3: Lines 823-825: NTILE with ORDER BY -- SQL Server sorts NULLs FIRST in ASC, LAST in DESC. Must use .asc_nulls_first() for line 823 and .desc_nulls_last() for lines 824-825
Line 824: NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY cf.OrderCount DESC) -> F.ntile(5).over(Window.orderBy(F.col('OrderCount').desc_nulls_last()))
Line 825: NTILE(5) OVER (ORDER BY cf.TotalRevenue_PathA DESC) -> F.ntile(5).over(Window.orderBy(F.col('TotalRevenue_PathA').desc_nulls_last()))
Line 822: cf.* -> use customer_facts_df.columns list in select, then add window columns with withColumn
Line 829: Index -> .repartition('CustomerID').cache()
STEP 8 - Region Aggregates (lines 834-846):
Line 834: OBJECT_ID check -> skip
Lines 838-841: AVG(CONVERT(decimal(19,4), ...)) -> F.avg(F.col('TotalRevenue_PathA').cast(DecimalType(19, 4))).alias('RegionAvgRevenue') etc.
Line 846: Index -> .repartition('RegionName').cache()
STEP 9 - JSON Payload (lines 851-899):
Lines 853-877: FOR JSON PATH with nested subquery -> Two approaches:

Approach A (recommended): Build the JSON struct directly. For topCategories (lines 865-873), pre-aggregate top 5 categories per customer using window: rank by SUM(LineTotal) DESC per CustomerID, filter rank <= 5, then F.collect_list(F.struct('category', 'revenue')). Then build outer struct with F.struct() and F.to_json()
Lines 865-873: TOP(5) subquery per customer -> use Window.partitionBy('CustomerID').orderBy(F.col('revenue').desc()) with F.row_number(), filter <= 5, then F.collect_list(F.struct(...))
Line 874: FOR JSON PATH, WITHOUT_ARRAY_WRAPPER -> F.to_json(F.struct(...))

Lines 883-897: CROSS APPLY OPENJSON -> Since we build the JSON ourselves, skip JSON round-trip entirely. Instead, keep the struct columns directly and alias them as revenueA_json, revenueB_json, orders_json, rfmScore_json. This is more efficient and avoids parse overhead

If JSON round-trip must be preserved for fidelity: F.from_json(F.col('PayloadJson'), schema) with schema = StructType([StructField('revenueA', StringType()), StructField('revenueB', StringType()), StructField('orders', StringType()), StructField('rfmScore', StringType())])
Lines 885-888: TRY_CONVERT -> .cast() (PySpark returns NULL on cast failure, same as TRY_CONVERT)
STEP 10 - QC Rules / Dynamic SQL Cursor (lines 905-943):

Lines 907-913: CREATE TABLE #QCFail -> define schema: qc_fail_schema = StructType([StructField('CheckGroup', StringType()), StructField('CheckName', StringType()), StructField('Severity', ByteType()), StructField('Failed', BooleanType())])
Lines 918-943: CURSOR + sp_executesql -> THIS IS THE MAJOR LIMITATION
TODO: Lines 918-943: Cursor with dynamic SQL. The QC predicates are stored as SQL strings in dbo.AWLT_QC_Definition and executed via sp_executesql. Two options:
Option A (if predicates are known/finite): Build a predicate_map dict mapping CheckName to Python lambda/function that evaluates against DataFrames. Iterate and evaluate.
Option B (if predicates are truly dynamic): Use spark.sql() to evaluate each predicate string after translating SQL syntax. Mark with TODO for manual review.

Recommended pattern: qc_definitions = spark.table('awlt_qc_definition').orderBy('QCId').limit(60).collect(); results = []; for row in qc_definitions: # TODO: Manual review -- dynamic SQL predicate evaluation. Original predicates must be translated to PySpark SQL or DataFrame API
Line 932: DECLARE @tmp TABLE(FailFlag int) -> not needed if using spark.sql() approach

Line 948: EXEC log -> logger.log_step('END', 'SUCCESS', 'Completed', complete=True)

Line 976: (r.R_Score + r.F_Score + r.M_Score) -> (F.col('R_Score') + F.col('F_Score') + F.col('M_Score')).alias('RFM_Score')

Lines 977-978: CASE WHEN RevenuePctDelta_AB IS NULL -> F.when(F.col('RevenuePctDelta_AB').isNull(), 0).when(F.col('RevenuePctDelta_AB') <= params.revenue_tolerance_pct, 1).otherwise(0).alias('QC_Pass_Revenue_AB')
Line 983: NULLIF(ra.RegionAvgRevenue, 0) -> use _nullif_zero helper pattern

Lines 990-991: Scalar subqueries COUNT from #QCFail -> pre-compute: qc_sev3 = qc_fail_df.where((F.col('Failed') == True) & (F.col('Severity') == 3)).count(); qc_sev2 = ...; then add as literal columns with F.lit()
Lines 992-997: Param columns -> .withColumn('SnapshotStartDate', F.lit(start_date)) etc.
Line 997: @RunId -> F.lit(run_id)

Lines 1002-1006: ORDER BY with DESC -> .orderBy(F.col('RegionName').asc_nulls_first(), (F.col('R_Score') + F.col('F_Score') + F.col('M_Score')).desc_nulls_last(), F.col('TotalRevenue_PathA').desc_nulls_last(), F.col('CustomerName').asc_nulls_first())
GOTCHA #3: Lines 1002-1006: ORDER BY -- apply correct null ordering to match SQL Server defaults
Lines 1008-1014: END TRY / BEGIN CATCH -> except Exception as exc: msg = f'Error: {exc}'; logger.log_step('END', 'FAIL', msg, complete=True); raise
Line 1011: CONCAT('Error ', ERROR_NUMBER(), ...) -> f-string with str(exc)
Line 1013: THROW -> raise (re-raise the caught exception)
CLEANUP: After final result is returned, call .unpersist() on all cached DataFrames: orders_df, lines_df, agg_a_df, agg_b_df, customer_facts_df, rfm_df, region_agg_df
gotchas_relevant: #2 Case-sensitive string comparison (line 681 RegionFilter, line 674 string literals)
#3 NULL ordering in ORDER BY (lines 823-825 NTILE windows, lines 1002-1006 final ORDER BY)
#9 COUNT(*) vs COUNT(col) -- ensure COUNT_BIG(*) maps to F.count('*') not F.count('col') (lines 724, 752, 990-991)
limitations_found: Cursor-based dynamic SQL QC evaluation at lines 918-943 -- cannot auto-convert sp_executesql with runtime predicates from AWLT_QC_Definition table. Insert TODO with predicate-map pattern suggestion.
FOR JSON PATH with nested correlated subquery at lines 865-874 -- replace with pre-aggregation + collect_list + to_json pattern
CROSS APPLY OPENJSON at lines 891-897 -- can be optimized away by keeping struct columns directly instead of JSON round-trip
usp_AWLT_OneCSV_ConverterTest_1k.strategy: Break into 11 sub-steps matching SQL sections. Convert steps 1-9 and 11 directly. Step 10 (QC cursor+dynamic SQL) requires TODO and manual review. Add validation comments for decimal precision. Cache aggressively since temp tables are reused across steps.
